1. The number of 0s is exactly 'zero'.
2. The number of 1s is exactly 'one'.
3. Every subarray of size greater than 'limit' must contain both 0 and 1.

public int numberOfStableArrays(int zero, int one, int limit) {

   int MOD = 1_000_000_007;

   int maxN = Math.max(zero, one);

            dp[n][m] = (n <= limit) ? 1 : 0;

         } else {

            int sum =0;

            int maxI = Math.min(limit, n - (m-1));

            for (int i=1; i<=maxI; i++) {

               if (sum >= MOD) sum -= MOD;

            }

            dp[n][m] = sum % MOD;

         }

      }

   }

   int total =0;

   for (int k0=1; k0<=zero; k0++) {

      for (int k1=1; k1<=one; k1++) {

         if (Math.abs(k0 -k1) >1) continue;

         int ways0 = dp[zero][k0];

         int ways1 = dp[one][k1];

         if (ways0 ==0 || ways1 ==0) continue;

         if (k0 ==k1) {

            total = (total + 2 * ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         } else if (k0 == k1 +1) {

            total = (total + ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         } else if (k1 == k0 +1) {

            total = (total + ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         }

      }

   }

   return total % MOD;

}

So, the code outline is:

public int numberOfStableArrays(int zero, int one, int limit) {

   int MOD = 1_000_000_007;

   int maxN = Math.max(zero, one);

   int[][] dp = new int[maxN +1][maxN +1];

   for (int n=1; n<=maxN; n++) {

      for (int m=1; m<=n; m++) {

         if (m ==1) {

            dp[n][m] = (n <= limit) ? 1 : 0;

         } else {

            int sum =0;

            int maxI = Math.min(limit, n - (m-1));

            for (int i=1; i<=maxI; i++) {

               sum += dp[n-i][m-1];

               if (sum >= MOD) sum -= MOD;

            }

            dp[n][m] = sum % MOD;

         }

      }

   }

   int total =0;

   for (int k0=1; k0<=zero; k0++) {

      for (int k1=1; k1<=one; k1++) {

         if (Math.abs(k0 -k1) >1) continue;

         int ways0 = dp[zero][k0];

         int ways1 = dp[one][k1];

         if (ways0 ==0 || ways1 ==0) continue;

         if (k0 ==k1) {

            total = (total + 2 * ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         } else if (k0 == k1 +1) {

            total = (total + ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         } else if (k1 == k0 +1) {

            total = (total + ways0 * ways1) % MOD;

         }

      }

   }

   return total % MOD;

}

Wait, but in the code, the dp is computed for all n up to maxN, which is the maximum of zero and one.

But for the cases where n is larger than the given zero or one, but in the loops for k0 and k1, we only go up to zero and one, respectively.
